Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

However, practicality is poor: Recipe 1 requires a long list of unavailable ingredients for a 'simple dinner for one,' and Recipe 2 explicitly doesn't use the stated ingredients (salmon/asparagus) and requires steak which the user doesn't have. assert.value:
Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

vars.input: I have chicken and broccoli. Please help me prepare dinner for two people.

results.error: The recipes require numerous non-staple, non-provided ingredients. The Gochujang Steak Bowl uses the user's ingredients but still needs gochujang, sesame oil, cucumber, green onions, and sesame seeds. The Cumin Beef & Hummus Pita ignores egg and rice entirely and requires pita, hummus, feta, pickled beets, and lemon juice. The Za'atar Chicken Bowl ignores beef and egg, requires a completely different protein (chicken), and loads up on specialty spices and toppings. Overall, the recipes are dominated by unlisted non-staple ingredients.

componentResults.reason: The recipes require numerous non-staple, non-provided ingredients. The Gochujang Steak Bowl uses the user's ingredients but still needs gochujang, sesame oil, cucumber, green onions, and sesame seeds. The Cumin Beef & Hummus Pita ignores egg and rice entirely and requires pita, hummus, feta, pickled beets, and lemon juice. The Za'atar Chicken Bowl ignores beef and egg, requires a completely different protein (chicken), and loads up on specialty spices and toppings. Overall, the recipes are dominated by unlisted non-staple ingredients.

Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

Return the score.

Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Slowly bring up temperature while tossing pasta. Can come to light simmer without breaking. 6. Serve pasta, finish with extra sauce, grated parm and reserved guanciale.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"recipe_card","data":{"event_type":"recipe_card","recipe":{"id":"r010","name":"Chicken and Ginger Stir Fry","name_zh":"姜丝炒鸡","cuisine":"Asian","cooking_method":"stir-fry","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["aromatic","tangy","savory"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["chicken thigh"],"veggie":["ginger","garlic","broccoli"],"sauce":["soy sauce","rice vinegar","honey"],"carb":["rice"]},"ingredients_have":["rice vinegar","broccoli","rice"],"ingredients_need":["chicken thigh","ginger","garlic","soy sauce","honey","vegetable oil"],"ingredients":[{"name":"chicken thigh","amount":"1 lb","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"ginger","amount":"2 inches","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"3 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"soy sauce","amount":"2 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"rice vinegar","amount":"1 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"honey","amount":"1 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"broccoli","amount":"1 cup","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"rice","amount":"for serving","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"vegetable oil","amount":"2 tbsp","pcsv":[]}],"instructions":"Slice chicken thigh into strips. Prep the noodles and sauce: Soak noodles to rehydrate per package directions. Mix all sauce ingredients in a container until well combined. 2. Stir fry the eggs and vegetables: Heat wok or large pan over high heat with oil. Add beaten egg, swirl around pan until fluffy with browning, chop and set aside. Add more oil, garlic, and white pepper; bloom for 30-45 seconds. Add chopped broccolini, stir fry 1-2 minutes, add a drizzle of sauce, then remove to bowl with eggs. 3. Brown the chicken: Add oil to wok, add chicken slices, brown 2 minutes per side, add sauce for color and seasoning. 4. Bring it all together: Add broccolini and egg back to wok with drained noodles. Add enough sauce to coat, stir fry 5-7 minutes until noodles soften and absorb sauce. Soak the noodles in hot water according to package directions. Stir tamarind paste, fish sauce, palm sugar, and soy sauce until sugar dissolves.\n2. Set wok over high heat with oil. Sear protein until browned and cooked through. Remove and set aside. Add oil, crack in eggs, scramble until cooked, set aside with protein. Wipe wok.\n3. Drain noodles. Add oil to wok, add dried shrimp and bloom 30 seconds until fragrant. Add garlic and ginger, stir fry 15-30 seconds. Add noodles, reserved egg and protein, and sauce. Toss until coated.\n4. Add scallions and bamboo shoots in final minute. Taste and adjust with fish sauce, sugar, or lime. Serve with peanuts, lime wedges, and green onions.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r074","name":"Thai Chile Pork & Cabbage Stir Fry","name_zh":"泰式辣椒猪肉炒卷心菜","cuisine":"Thai","cooking_method":"stir-fry","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["aromatic","spicy","umami"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["ground pork","eggs"],"veggie":["cabbage","thai chilies","shallots"],"sauce":["salt","cooking oil","fish sauce","msg","sesame oil","black sesame seeds"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["ground pork","cabbage","salt","thai chilies","shallots","cooking oil","fish sauce","msg","sesame oil","eggs","black sesame seeds"],"ingredients":[{"name":"ground pork","amount":"225 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"cabbage","amount":"1/4, thinly sliced","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"thai chilies","amount":"3-8, thinly sliced","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"shallots","amount":"1, thinly sliced","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cooking oil","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"fish sauce","amount":"a spoonful","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"msg","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"sesame oil","amount":"a drizzle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"eggs","amount":"1, fried","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"black sesame seeds","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Brown the pork: Preheat a pan over medium-high heat. Add the ground pork and press it into the hot pan, making a thin even layer across the hot surface. Allow the pork to sear undisturbed until the bottom begins to brown and take on some color. Using a potato masher, fork, or spatula, break up the pork into small pebble-sized bits. 2. Add the cabbage and steam: Once the pork is mostly cooked through, add in the sliced cabbage. Stir the cabbage into the meat to ensure nothing is burning on the bottom of the pan. Cover to steam and soften the cabbage for a minute or two. Remove the cover. Sauté for another minute or two to get more browning on the meat and edges of the cabbage and evaporate any excess moisture. 3. Finish with aromatics and sauces: Push the cabbage and pork mixture to one side of the pan. Add a drizzle of oil to the open space and add in the shallot and Thai chilies to bloom for about 30 seconds before mixing everything together. Add the fish sauce to deglaze any browning from the bottom of the pan. Turn off the heat. Add a drizzle of sesame oil and a sprinkle of MSG. Taste and adjust as needed. 4. Plate and serve: Serve with a fried egg and a sprinkle of sesame seeds for garnish.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"explanation","data":{"event_type":"explanation","text":"You’ve got the core Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. tests.description: C3: vague input — no ingredients, no party size

assert.value:

(3) Usefulness: if assumptions were made, is the output still usable? If clarifications were asked, are they the right ones (ingredients on hand, party size, dietary constraints)?

tests.description: D3: recipes swap with 2-turn history — vegetarian swap

assert.value:
Context: user asked for 3 dinners with chicken/rice/vegetables for 4, then asked to swap the first recipe for something vegetarian. Judge the FINAL response only.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, return the minimum:
(1) Swap executed: recipe 1 has been REPLACED (not added) and is clearly vegetarian (no meat/poultry/fish).
(2) Count preserved: total recipes = 3 (not 4, not 2).
(3) Continuity: recipes 2 and 3 are the same or consistent with the previous turn (the user only asked to change recipe 1).
Return the overall score.
